_metadata.description: Comprehensive IEEPA tariff rate database for tariff refund calculator. Covers all rate changes from Feb 2025 through SCOTUS termination Feb 2026 and Section 122 replacement.
_metadata.legalAuthority: International Emergency Economic Powers Act (50 U.S.C. 1701 et seq.), Trade Act of 1974 Section 122 (19 U.S.C. 2132)
sources: White House Annex I and Annex II
Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump (SCOTUS, Feb 20, 2026)

rates.name: Fentanyl Surcharge - China (Initial 10%)
rates.notes: Initial 10% fentanyl surcharge on products of PRC. EO 14195 signed Feb 1, 2025. Effective 12:01 AM EST Feb 4, 2025. Declared fentanyl flow from PRC an unusual and extraordinary threat.

rates.name: Fentanyl Surcharge - China (Escalated 20%)
rates.notes: Fentanyl tariff doubled to 20%. EO 14228 signed Mar 3, 2025. Effective 12:01 AM EST Mar 4, 2025. China had not taken adequate steps to address opioid crisis.

rates.name: Fentanyl Surcharge - China (Reduced 10%)
rates.notes: Fentanyl tariff reduced from 20% to 10% per US-China trade arrangement reached Oct 30 in Busan, South Korea (Xi-Trump meeting). EO signed Nov 4, 2025. Effective 12:01 AM EST Nov 10, 2025.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - China (Initial 34%)
rates.notes: Liberation Day reciprocal tariff. EO 14257 signed Apr 2, 2025. 34% additional tariff on China effective Apr 9, 2025. Stacked on top of 20% fentanyl = 54% total IEEPA.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - China (Escalated 84%)
rates.notes: China reciprocal escalated to 84% in response to Chinese retaliation. EO 14259 signed Apr 8, 2025. Effective 12:01 AM EDT Apr 9, 2025. Total IEEPA with fentanyl = 104%.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - China (Escalated 125%)
rates.notes: China reciprocal raised to 125% after further Chinese retaliation. EO 14266 signed Apr 9, 2025. Effective Apr 10, 2025. Total IEEPA with 20% fentanyl = 145%.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - China (Geneva De-escalation 10%)
rates.notes: Geneva trade talks (May 12) reduced reciprocal from 125% to 10% for 90 days. Effective 12:01 AM EDT May 14, 2025. Fentanyl 20% remains = 30% total IEEPA.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - China (90-day Extension at 10%)
rates.notes: President extended China tariff truce by another 90 days to Nov 10, 2025. EO signed Aug 11, 2025. Fentanyl 20% remains = 30% total IEEPA.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - China (1-Year Extension at 10%)
rates.notes: Per Busan Xi-Trump deal, reciprocal 10% extended for 1 year to Nov 10, 2026. Combined with fentanyl reduction to 10% = 20% total IEEPA. Terminated early by SCOTUS.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Canada (25% / 10% Energy)
rates.notes: EO 14193 signed Feb 1, 2025. Originally effective Feb 4 but paused 30 days after Canadian border commitments. Took effect 12:01 AM EST Mar 4, 2025. Energy/energy resources at 10% under 9903.01.24. All goods subject, no USMCA exemption initially.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Canada Energy (10%)
rates.notes: Reduced 10% rate for Canadian energy and energy resources. Same EO 14193. Effective Mar 4, 2025.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Canada Non-USMCA (25%)
rates.notes: Mar 6 amendment: USMCA-compliant goods exempted at 0% (9903.01.26). Non-USMCA goods remain at 25%. Effective 12:01 AM EST Mar 7, 2025.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Canada Non-USMCA Energy (10%)
rates.notes: Non-USMCA Canadian energy at 10%. Potash also at 10% regardless of USMCA status.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Canada Non-USMCA (Escalated 35%)
rates.notes: EO signed Jul 31, 2025. Canada tariff raised from 25% to 35% effective Aug 1, 2025. Anti-transshipment surcharge of 40% for goods routed through Canada to evade. USMCA goods remain exempt.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Canada Non-USMCA Energy (10%)
rates.notes: Canadian energy rate remained at 10% even after general rate escalation to 35%.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Mexico (25%)
rates.notes: EO 14194 signed Feb 1, 2025. Originally effective Feb 4 but paused 30 days. Took effect 12:01 AM EST Mar 4, 2025. 25% on ALL Mexican goods including USMCA initially.

rates.name: Fentanyl Tariff - Mexico Non-USMCA (25%)
rates.notes: Mar 6 amendment: USMCA-compliant goods exempted at 0% (9903.01.27). Non-USMCA goods remain at 25%. Potash at 10% regardless of USMCA. Effective 12:01 AM EST Mar 7, 2025. Remained at 25% through SCOTUS termination (not escalated like Canada).

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - Global Baseline (10%)
rates.notes: Liberation Day baseline tariff. EO 14257 signed Apr 2, 2025. 10% on all imports not otherwise covered by higher country-specific rates. Effective 12:01 AM EDT Apr 5, 2025. Canada, Mexico, and Section 232 goods exempt.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - Country-Specific Pause to 10%
rates.notes: On Apr 9, 2025, after market turmoil, Trump paused country-specific reciprocal rates above 10% for 90 days for all Annex I countries EXCEPT China. All Annex I countries (excl China) reverted to 10% baseline. Effective 12:01 AM EDT Apr 10, 2025.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - Country-Specific Pause Extended
rates.notes: Pause extended to Aug 1, 2025. EO extending modification signed early July 2025.

rates.name: Reciprocal Tariff - Country-Specific Rates Resume (Modified)
rates.notes: Country-specific rates came into effect Aug 7, 2025 (delayed from Aug 1). Many rates were modified from original Annex I via tariff letters and bilateral deals. See country-specific entries below. Countries without deals or letters reverted to original Annex I rates.

rates.name: SCOTUS IEEPA Termination
rates.notes: Supreme Court ruled 6-3 on Feb 20, 2026 that IEEPA does not authorize tariffs. All IEEPA tariffs (fentanyl, reciprocal, Russian oil, Brazil) terminated effective 12:00 AM ET Feb 24, 2026. CBP ceased collection.

rates.name: Section 122 Global Surcharge (10%)
rates.notes: Replacement tariff under Section 122 of Trade Act of 1974 (19 USC 2132). 10% global surcharge. Effective 12:01 AM ET Feb 24, 2026. Max 150 days, expires Jul 24, 2026. Max statutory rate is 15%. As of Apr 2026, rate remains 10% - Trump announced intent to raise to 15% on Feb 22 via social media but no formal proclamation issued. Same Annex II exclusions apply (USMCA goods, Section 232 products, critical minerals, pharmaceuticals, semiconductors, certain electronics).

liberationDayAnnexI.notes: Country-specific rates above 10% were in effect only Apr 9 before being paused on Apr 10. Resumed Aug 7, 2025 with modified rates for some countries. All terminated Feb 24, 2026 by SCOTUS.

countries.modifiedNotes: Reduced to 10% via Geneva deal, extended through SCOTUS termination
Tariff letter: 25% on Indian goods effective Aug 27, 2025
Japan-US deal: reciprocal rate set at 15%
Korea-US deal: reciprocal rate set at 15%

baselineOnlyCountries.notes: UK secured 10% deal; never subject to higher rate

section232Exemptions.description: Products subject to Section 232 tariffs are EXEMPT from IEEPA tariffs (both fentanyl and reciprocal). This is a critical deduction in the refund calculator.

categories.notes: 25% tariff on steel imports under Section 232. Exempt from IEEPA. No stacking.
categories.additionalNotes: Increased to 50% on some origins (e.g., imports from countries that did not negotiate quotas)

categories.notes: 25% tariff on aluminum imports under Section 232. Exempt from IEEPA. No stacking.

25% tariff on passenger vehicles, light trucks, and auto parts under Proclamation 10908 (Mar 26, 2025). Exempt from IEEPA reciprocal tariffs. USMCA-eligible vehicles: US content at 0%, non-US content at 25%.

htsBreakdown.description: Covered passenger vehicles and light trucks
USMCA non-US content of passenger vehicles

categories.notes: Section 232 investigation announced. Copper excluded from IEEPA reciprocal tariffs via Annex II regardless.

section232Exemptions.antiStackingRule: Executive Order of April 29, 2025 confirmed Section 232 tariffs take precedence over reciprocal tariffs. No stacking of IEEPA on top of 232. However, Section 232 steel/aluminum CAN stack with Section 232 auto tariffs where both apply.

productExclusions.description: Products excluded from IEEPA reciprocal tariffs via Annex II to EO 14257. These products are NOT subject to the reciprocal tariff and should be deducted from any refund calculation.

categories.description: Generic pharmaceuticals, generic pharmaceutical ingredients, and generic pharmaceutical chemical precursors
categories.notes: Broad pharma exclusion; includes APIs and chemical precursors

categories.description: Semiconductors, semiconductor manufacturing equipment, smartphones, laptops, memory chips, flat panel displays
categories.notes: Electronics exemption expanded in April 2025. Separate Section 232 investigation announced for semiconductors.

categories.description: Nickel, tin and thorium ores, rare earth elements, permanent magnets, LEDs
categories.notes: Critical minerals exclusion covers approximately 50 HTS codes

categories.description: Crude oil, natural gas, petroleum products, uranium, and related energy commodities

categories.notes: Full copper exclusion via Annex II
IEEPA statute itself exempts informational materials (Berman Amendment)

categories.description: Food, clothing, and medicine donated for humanitarian purposes

categories.notes: Global exception added for civil aviation products

categories.description: Products for personal use in accompanied baggage

htsCodeReference.description: Key IEEPA and Section 122 HTS Chapter 99 codes for tariff classification

reciprocalCodes.9903.01.32: Reciprocal tariff exclusion - Annex II products
reciprocalCodes.9903.01.33: Reciprocal tariff exclusion - Section 232 covered goods

section122Codes.9903.03.09: Section 122 exemption - country-specific trade deal goods

irsInterestRates.description: IRS quarterly interest rates for calculating refund interest under 19 USC 1505. Customs duty overpayment/underpayment interest is calculated per IRC 6621/6622, compounded daily.

capePhase1.description: Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries (CAPE) Phase 1 - IEEPA Duty Refund Program
capePhase1.legalBasis: Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump (SCOTUS Feb 20, 2026); CIT order Mar 4, 2026
capePhase1.refundScope: IEEPA duties ONLY. Section 232, Section 301, and other duties on same entry are NOT refundable.

refundPeriod.description: All IEEPA duties collected between Feb 4, 2025 and Feb 23, 2026 are refundable

included.name: Consumption - AD/CVD
included.notes: Eligible if not pending Commerce liquidation instructions

included.name: Informal
included.notes: Informal entries below formal entry threshold

included.name: Warehouse Entry
included.notes: Warehouse entries where IEEPA Chapter 99 codes were declared

liquidationStatus: Unliquidated entries (status: open, suspended, extended, under review)
Entries liquidated within preceding 80 days of CAPE submission
Entries with liquidation status 'suspended' are accepted but not immediately processed
additionalRequirements: Entry must be filed in ACE with a liquidation status visible in ACE
Entry must have declared IEEPA Chapter 99 HTS codes
Entry must NOT be covered by an open or suspended protest
Entry must NOT be flagged for reconciliation
Entry must NOT be on an active drawback claim

refundCalculation.principal: Total IEEPA duties collected on the entry
refundCalculation.interest: Calculated per 19 USC 1505(c) using IRS quarterly rates (IRC 6621/6622), compounded daily, from date of duty deposit to date of refund
refundCalculation.deductions: None - full IEEPA duty amount is refundable

processingTimeline.description: CBP targets 45-day processing for Phase 1 refunds from date of CAPE submission

preservingRefundRights.description: For entries NOT eligible for Phase 1, importers must take action to preserve refund rights
actions: File a protest (19 USC 1514) within 180 days of liquidation
Request voluntary reliquidation within 90 days of liquidation
If reconciliation-flagged, file reconciliation entry to trigger liquidation
If on drawback, may need to withdraw drawback claim first

calculatorLogic.description: Rules for computing IEEPA tariff refund amounts

steps.action: Determine entry date (date goods entered for consumption or withdrawn from warehouse)
steps.notes: This determines which rate schedule applies
Affects which tariff type(s) apply (fentanyl, reciprocal, or both)
If product is steel, aluminum, or auto/auto parts subject to Section 232, it is EXEMPT from IEEPA. No refund for Section 232 duties.

If product is on the Annex II exclusion list (pharmaceuticals, semiconductors, copper, lumber, energy, critical minerals, etc.), it was not subject to reciprocal tariffs. Verify whether IEEPA duty was actually collected.
USMCA-compliant goods from Canada and Mexico were exempt from IEEPA fentanyl tariffs starting Mar 7, 2025. No IEEPA duty should have been collected on USMCA goods after that date.

steps.action: Calculate applicable IEEPA rate(s) based on entry date
steps.notes: Look up the rate(s) in the rates array. For China: may have both fentanyl + reciprocal stacking. For Canada/Mexico: fentanyl only (not subject to reciprocal). For other countries: reciprocal only.
Refund = sum of all IEEPA duties collected. Section 301, Section 232, and regular MFN duties are NOT refundable.

steps.action: Compute interest: daily compounding at IRS quarterly rate from deposit date to refund date
steps.notes: Use irsInterestRates.quarters to determine applicable rate for each day in the accrual period

china.description: China entries may have BOTH fentanyl and reciprocal IEEPA tariffs stacking
china.example: Entry on Jun 1, 2025: 20% fentanyl (9903.01.20) + 10% reciprocal (9903.01.63) = 30% total IEEPA

canadaMexico.description: Canada/Mexico have fentanyl tariffs ONLY (not subject to reciprocal tariffs per EO 14257 exemption)
canadaMexico.example: Canada non-USMCA entry on Sep 1, 2025: 35% fentanyl (9903.01.22) only

otherCountries.description: All other countries have reciprocal tariff ONLY (no fentanyl tariff)
otherCountries.example: Vietnam entry on Sep 1, 2025: 46% reciprocal (9903.01.25) only
